title: Chicken Pasta Primavera
description: Throw some veggies together in a heavy cream based parmesan sauce.
ingredients: ½ tsp Better Than Bouillion mixed with ½ cup of water (normal chicken broth works too)
instructions: Place chicken breasts on a cutting board, cover in plastic wrap and pound the thicker side until breast is an even thickness, about ½ inch thick. Sprinkle half of the seasoning mix to both sides of the chicken breasts.
Mix the seasoning in a small bowl (1 tsp dried oregano, 1 tsp kosher salt, ½ tsp garlic powder, ½ tsp black pepper)

Heat a 12" skillet over medium heat, add olive oil. While pan is heating, fill a large pot of water and begin boiling on high heat. Place breast on the heated pan presentation side down first, cook about 3½ minutes per side, check with meat thermometer for doneness you aren't Gordon Ramsey
Once water is boiling, add salt. Wait for water to get back up to a boil then add the pasta.
Add ½ cup of water to a small pot and boil, adding ½ tsp of Better Than Boullion once the water begins to boil.

Once chicken is cooked add to a plate and cover with foil. Let pan cool slightly then melt butter, being careful not to burn (remove from burner temporarily if needed). Add minced garlic to melted butter and sauté for 30 seconds or until fragrant.
Add the sliced tomatoes and zucchini, sauté for 2 minutes. Add the remaining seasoning mix then pour in chicken broth. Stir together and cook an additional 2 minutes.

Now add 1/2 cup parmesan cheese and 1 cup heavy cream, stirring together to combine. Add ½ cup frozen peas in (roughly 85g) and cook an additional 1-2 minutes, add the pasta and mix everything together, then turn the heat off.
Slice the cooked chicken breasts and divide to 4 equal portions. Store half of each breast in seperate containers.
Tare out a large metal bowl and add the finished pasta. Divide into four equal portions. Top the pasta with the sliced chicken breast finish with a pinch of sea salt.
